HEADING: LTI Configuration
ACCESS_DENIED_MESSAGE: Your current package does not include LTI integration.
ACCESS_DENIED_DESCRIPTION: LTI integration enables FastComments commenting within LMS courses via the LTI 1.3 standard. You may need to contact support to enable this feature.
VALIDATION_PLATFORM_URL_INVALID: The Platform URL is not a valid URL.
VALIDATION_PLATFORM_URL_HTTPS_REQUIRED: The Platform URL must use HTTPS.
VALIDATION_PLATFORM_URL_PRIVATE_IP: The Platform URL must not resolve to a private or reserved IP address.
VALIDATION_PLATFORM_URL_DNS_FAILED: Could not resolve the Platform URL hostname. Please check the URL and try again.
VALIDATION_AT_LEAST_ONE_PLACEMENT: At least one placement must be enabled.
STEP1_DESCRIPTION: Start by naming your configuration and entering your LMS platform URL.
STEP2_DESCRIPTION: Use the Configuration URL below to set up a Developer Key in your LMS, then enter the Client ID it generates.
STEP2_URL_INSTRUCTION: Copy this URL and paste it into your LMS Developer Key setup:
STEP2_LMS_STEP_1: Go to Admin → Developer Keys in your Canvas LMS.
STEP2_LMS_STEP_3: In the Redirect URIs field, paste the Launch URL shown below.
STEP2_LMS_STEP_4: On the right, select "Enter URL" as the configuration method and paste the Configuration URL into the JSON URL field.
STEP2_LMS_STEP_5: Give the key a name (e.g. "FastComments") and click Save.
STEP2_LMS_STEP_6: Set the key to ON and click Allow when prompted.
STEP2_LMS_STEP_7: Copy the number in the Details column — that is your Client ID.
STEP2_REDIRECT_URI_INSTRUCTION: Enter this URL in the Redirect URIs field when creating your Developer Key:
STEP2_CLIENT_ID_DESCRIPTION: Paste the Client ID from your LMS below.
STEP3_DESCRIPTION: Review your configuration and enable the integration.
COMPLETE_DESCRIPTION: Your LTI integrations are listed below. You can edit configurations or add new deployments.
HELP_CONFIG_NAME: A label to identify this deployment. Useful if you have multiple LMS instances.
HELP_PLATFORM_URL: The base URL of your LMS instance (e.g. https://yourschool.instructure.com).
HELP_CLIENT_ID: The Client ID from the LTI Developer Key you created for FastComments.
HELP_DEPLOYMENT_ID: Optional. The deployment ID assigned by your LMS when you add the external tool.
CONFIRM_DELETE: Are you sure you want to delete this LTI configuration?
ROLE_MAPPING_DESCRIPTION: LTI roles are automatically mapped to FastComments permissions:
DOCUMENTATION_TEXT: Need help setting up LTI? Check out our Canvas LMS Setup Guide or visit our LTI landing page for other LMS platforms, or contact support for assistance.
HELP_PLACEMENTS: Choose where FastComments appears in your Canvas courses.
HELP_PLACEMENTS_MANAGEMENT: After changing placements here, also update the Placements section in your Canvas Developer Key to match.
HELP_PLACEMENT_EDITOR_BUTTON: Instructors can embed FastComments in Pages, Quizzes, and Announcements via the Rich Content Editor toolbar.
